perturbation.original_text: 6.7. The Counterparty must inform Eskom i n writing of any existing or potential conflict of interest with the interests of Eskom pursuant to this Project . Where any potential or actual conflict of interests exists, the prospective f irm shall immediately notify Eskom thereof in writing. Eskom at its sole discretion may elect whether the Counterparty can continue with the Project fully or partially and to the extent the Counterparty cannot continue with the Project it will renounce any benefits it may have obtained had it completed the Project and will be paid only for work completed. The omission of such information or the making of any false statements or representations shall entitle Eskom to terminate all contracts with the Counterparty regarding the Project and to invoke all remedies available to it in law .
perturbation.changed_text:
23. PUBLICITY
Neither Party will make or issue any formal or informal announcement or statement to the press or any Third Party in connection with this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.

6.7. The Counterparty must inform Eskom i n writing of any existing or potential conflict of interest with the interests of Eskom pursuant to this Project . Where any potential or actual conflict of interests exists, the prospective f irm shall immediately notify Eskom thereof in writing. Eskom at its sole discretion may elect whether the Counterparty can continue with the Project fully or partially and to the extent the Counterparty cannot continue with the Project it will renounce any benefits it may have obtained had it completed the Project and will be paid only for work completed. The omission of such information or the making of any false statements or representations shall entitle Eskom to terminate all contracts with the Counterparty regarding the Project and to invoke all remedies available to it in law .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the conflict of interest clause to a section about publicity makes it less prominent and could lead to a counterparty overlooking it. The correct disclosure helps ensure fair dealing and prevents corruption, and this relocation makes it harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act 12 of 2004 (South Africa)
perturbation.law_citation: Section 3(1)(a) of the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act 12 of 2004
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 3(1)(a) of the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act requires mandatory reporting of corruption. By burying the conflict-of-interest reporting requirement within a section about 'Publicity,' the contract reduces the prominence of this critical disclosure, potentially discouraging counterparties from reporting, thereby hindering compliance with the Act's objectives. This makes it less likely to be noticed, understood, and acted upon.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a structural flaw where the conflict of interest clause is moved to a section concerning publicity, which reduces its prominence. The Law Explanation states this hinders compliance with the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act, which requires mandatory reporting of corruption. The absence of relevant information in the scraped snippets does not negate the contradiction. Relocating a crucial disclosure requirement within an unrelated section like 'Publicity' makes it less likely to be noticed and acted upon, undermining the Act's objectives, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 19. GOVERNING LAW The Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of South Africa.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to the definition of confidential information makes it less visible. The governing law clause is critical for determining which jurisdiction's laws apply to the contract, and it needs to be easily located. This makes it harder to enforce, because if it's not easily seen, it may not be enforced as easily.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of Contract Interpretation in South Africa
perturbation.law_citation: Common Law Principles of Contract Interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: Although not codified in a single statute, established principles of contract interpretation in South Africa emphasize clarity and prominence of key terms like the governing law clause. By placing this clause within the 'Confidential Information' definition, it diminishes its prominence and accessibility, conflicting with the principle that such a crucial clause should be readily identifiable.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation and law explanation both describe how the change diminishes the prominence and accessibility of the governing law clause, which is a crucial aspect of the contract. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a required legal disclosure (governing law) is placed in an irrelevant section (definition of confidential information). This obscurity makes it more difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The fact that the law snippets are missing does not change the analysis given that the provided documentation is enough to suggest the contradiction.
